```
/* --------------------------------------------------------------------------
 *  Glyph palette styling.
 *  Compact grid of tiles, organised under section headers per glyph
 *  type. Hover lifts each tile slightly so the hit-target reads as
 *  interactive without being noisy.
 * ------------------------------------------------------------------------ */

.palette {
  width: 240px;
  flex-shrink: 0;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  background: #fefaf2;
  border-right: 1px solid #e5d8c1;
  overflow: hidden;
}

.palette__header {
  padding: 16px 16px 8px;
  border-bottom: 1px solid #e5d8c1;
  background: #fffaf2;
}

.palette__title {
  margin: 0;
  font-size: 14px;
  font-weight: 700;
  color: #5b4126;
  letter-spacing: 0.02em;
}

.palette__hint {
  margin: 4px 0 0;
  font-size: 11px;
  color: #b08d5e;
}

.palette__sections {
  flex: 1;
  overflow-y: auto;
  padding: 8px 0 16px;
}

.palette__section {
  padding: 12px 16px 4px;
}

.palette__section-title {
  margin: 0 0 8px;
  font-size: 11px;
  font-weight: 600;
  color: #b08d5e;
  text-transform: uppercase;
  letter-spacing: 0.06em;
}

.palette__grid {
  display: grid;
  grid-template-columns: repeat(3, 1fr);
  gap: 6px;
}

.palette__tile {
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  align-items: center;
  justify-content: center;
  padding: 8px 4px;
  background: #fff;
  border: 1px solid #e5d8c1;
  border-radius: 6px;
  cursor: pointer;
  transition: transform 80ms ease, background 80ms ease, border-color 80ms ease;
  color: #5b4126;
  gap: 4px;
}

.palette__tile:hover {
  background: #fff6e3;
  border-color: #d8a865;
  transform: translateY(-1px);
}

.palette__tile:active {
  transform: translateY(0);
  background: #fceac4;
}

.palette__char {
  font-size: 30px;
  line-height: 1;
  color: #1f2937;
}

.palette__code {
  font-size: 10px;
  color: #b08d5e;
  font-weight: 600;
}
```

## [999] assistant

**Write**: `/home/fox/workspace/lotus/enhanced/src/components/ToolbarButton.tsx`
```
/* --------------------------------------------------------------------------
 *  Shared toolbar button. Visual style is the same as the legacy
 *  editor — brown idle / lighter brown active — so the prototype reads
 *  as the same product family.
 * ------------------------------------------------------------------------ */

import type { ButtonHTMLAttributes, ReactNode } from "react";
import "./ToolbarButton.css";

interface IProps extends ButtonHTMLAttributes<HTMLButtonElement> {
  active?: boolean;
  children: ReactNode;
}

export const ToolbarButton = ({
  active = false,
  disabled = false,
  className,
  children,
  ...rest
}: IProps) => {
  // Suppress mousedown focus-steal so the editor selection stays
  // intact when the user clicks a toolbar button.
  return (
    <button
      type="button"
      disabled={disabled}
      onMouseDown={(e) => e.preventDefault()}
      className={[
        "tb-btn",
        active ? "tb-btn--active" : "",
        disabled ? "tb-btn--disabled" : "",
        className ?? "",
      ]
        .filter(Boolean)
        .join(" ")}
      {...rest}
    >
      {children}
    </button>
  );
};
```

## [1001] assistant

**Write**: `/home/fox/workspace/lotus/enhanced/src/components/Popover.tsx`
```
/* --------------------------------------------------------------------------
 *  Bare-bones popover used by the Group and Rotate pickers. Anchors
 *  beneath its trigger, closes on Esc / outside click. Kept simple so
 *  the prototype doesn't pull in a popper-style library.
 * ------------------------------------------------------------------------ */

import { useEffect, useRef, type ReactNode } from "react";
import "./Popover.css";

interface IProps {
  open: boolean;
  onClose: () => void;
  children: ReactNode;
  /** Anchor element — popover positions beneath this. */
  anchorRef: React.RefObject<HTMLElement | null>;
  /** Label for the popover heading. */
  label: string;
}

export const Popover = ({
  open,
  onClose,
  children,
  anchorRef,
  label,
}: IProps) => {
  const popoverRef = useRef<HTMLDivElement>(null);

  useEffect(() => {
    if (!open) return;
    const onMouseDown = (e: MouseEvent) => {
      const t = e.target as Node;
      if (popoverRef.current?.contains(t)) return;
      if (anchorRef.current?.contains(t)) return;
      onClose();
    };
    const onKey = (e: KeyboardEvent) => {
      if (e.key === "Escape") onClose();
    };
    document.addEventListener("mousedown", onMouseDown);
    document.addEventListener("keydown", onKey);
    return () => {
      document.removeEventListener("mousedown", onMouseDown);
      document.removeEventListener("keydown", onKey);
    };
  }, [open, onClose, anchorRef]);

  if (!open) return null;
  return (
    <div
      ref={popoverRef}
      role="dialog"
      aria-label={label}
      className="popover"
      onMouseDown={(e) => {
        // Don't let mousedown inside the popover steal editor focus.
        e.stopPropagation();
      }}
    >
      {children}
    </div>
  );
};
```

```
/* --------------------------------------------------------------------------
 *  Group orientation picker. Click "Group" → choose Vertical or
 *  Horizontal. Disabled when the selection has fewer than 2 characters
 *  (the minimum needed to form a group).
 * ------------------------------------------------------------------------ */

import { useRef, useState } from "react";
import { Popover } from "./Popover";
import { ToolbarButton } from "./ToolbarButton";
import type { GroupOrientation } from "../editor/types";
import "./GroupPicker.css";

interface IProps {
  disabled: boolean;
  onGroup: (orientation: GroupOrientation) => void;
}

interface Option {
  id: GroupOrientation;
  label: string;
  hint: string;
}

  {
    id: "vertical",
    label: "Vertical",
    hint: "Stack on top of each other",
  },
  {
    id: "horizontal",
    label: "Horizontal",
    hint: "Pack side-by-side",
  },
];

const Preview = ({ orientation }: { orientation: GroupOrientation }) => {
  const slot = (
    <span
      style={{
        background: "rgba(91, 65, 38, 0.18)",
        border: "1px solid rgba(91, 65, 38, 0.4)",
        borderRadius: 4,
        flex: 1,
      }}
    />
  );

    <span
      style={{
        display: "inline-flex",
        flexDirection: orientation === "vertical" ? "column" : "row",
        gap: 4,
        width: 44,
        height: 44,
        padding: 4,
        background: "white",
        border: "1px solid #d8a86555",
        borderRadius: 6,
      }}
    >

      {slot}

  );
};

      >

              key={opt.id}
              ty
```

```
/* --------------------------------------------------------------------------
 *  Rotation picker. Five quick presets (0, 90, 180, 270, custom) and a
 *  slider for fine-grained control. Applies the `rotation` mark to the
 *  current selection.
 * ------------------------------------------------------------------------ */

import { useRef, useState } from "react";
import { Popover } from "./Popover";
import { ToolbarButton } from "./ToolbarButton";
import "./RotatePicker.css";

interface IProps {
  disabled: boolean;
  currentAngle: number;
  onSetAngle: (angle: number) => void;
  onClear: () => void;
}

const PRESETS = [0, 90, 180, 270];

export const RotatePicker = ({
  disabled,
  currentAngle,
  onSetAngle,
  onClear,
}: IProps) => {
  const [open, setOpen] = useState(false);
  const [draft, setDraft] = useState<number>(currentAngle);
  const anchorRef = useRef<HTMLDivElement>(null);

  const commit = (angle: number) => {
    setDraft(angle);
    if (angle === 0) onClear();
    else onSetAngle(angle);
  };

  return (
    <div className="picker" ref={anchorRef}>
      <ToolbarButton
        active={currentAngle !== 0 || open}
        disabled={disabled}
        title={disabled ? "Select content to rotate" : "Rotate selection"}
        onClick={() => {
          setDraft(currentAngle);
          setOpen((v) => !v);
        }}
      >
        Rotate
      </ToolbarButton>
      <Popover
        open={open}
        onClose={() => setOpen(false)}
        anchorRef={anchorRef}
        label="Rotate selection"
      >
        <p className="popover__title">Rotate selection</p>
        <div className="rotate__presets">
          {PRESETS.map((p) => (
            <button
              key={p}
              type="button"
              className={`rotate__preset${draft === p ? " is-active" : ""}`}
              onMouseDown={(e) => e.preventDefault()}
              onClick={() => commit(p)}
            >
              {p}°
            </button>
          ))}
        </div>
        <label className="rotate__slider-label">
          Custom: {draft}°
          <input
            type="range"
            min={0}
            max={359}
            step={1}
            value={draft}
            onChange={(e) => setDraft(Number(e.target.value))}
            onMouseUp={() => commit(draft)}
            onTouchEnd={() => commit(draft)}
          />
        </label>
        <div className="rotate__actions">
          <button
            type="button"
            className="ro
```

```
/* --------------------------------------------------------------------------
 *  Top-of-editor toolbar.
 *
 *  Reads selection state from the TipTap editor via `useEditorState` so
 *  each button re-renders with the right active/disabled flags as the
 *  user moves the caret. Commands are dispatched via the standard
 *  TipTap chain — every action is a single line, every action goes
 *  through history.
 * ------------------------------------------------------------------------ */

import type { Editor } from "@tiptap/react";
import { useEditorState } from "@tiptap/react";
import { GroupPicker } from "./GroupPicker";
import { RotatePicker } from "./RotatePicker";
import { ToolbarButton } from "./ToolbarButton";
import { useVerticalMode } from "../editor/useVerticalMode";
import "./Toolbar.css";

interface IProps {
  editor: Editor | null;
  fontSize: number;
  setFontSize: (n: number) => void;
  direction: "ltr" | "rtl";
  setDirection: (d: "ltr" | "rtl") => void;
}

export const Toolbar = ({
  editor,
  fontSize,
  setFontSize,
  direction,
  setDirection,
}: IProps) => {
  const verticalMode = useVerticalMode(editor);

  // useEditorState gives us a snapshot of selection-driven state that
  // re-runs whenever the selection or doc changes. Cheap and reactive.
  const state = useEditorState({
    editor,
    selector: (ctx) => {
      const ed = ctx.editor;
      if (!ed) {
        return {
          bold: false,
          italic: false,
          canBold: false,
          canItalic: false,
          canUndo: false,
          canRedo: false,
          hasSelection: false,
          selectionLength: 0,
          inCartouche: false,
          inGroup: false,
          rotationAngle: 0,
        };
      }
      const { from, to, empty } = ed.state.selection;
      const hasSelection = !empty;
      // We need the raw character count to know whether grouping is
      // sensible (>= 2 chars). doc.textBetween is the cheapest way
      // to count.
      const text = ed.state.doc.textBetween(from, to, "");
      const rotationMark = ed.getAttributes("rotation");
      return {
        bold: ed.isActive("bold"),
        italic: ed.isActive("italic"),
        canBold: ed.can().toggleBold(),
        canItalic: ed.can().toggleItalic(),
        canUndo: ed.can().undo(),
        canRedo: ed.can().redo(),
        hasSelection,
        selectionLength: text.length,
        inCartouche: ed.isActive("cartouche"),
        inGroup: ed.isActive("glyphGroup"),
        rotat

_... [truncated 4415 chars] ..._
```

## [1010] assistant

**Write**: `/home/fox/workspace/lotus/enhanced/src/App.tsx`
```
/* --------------------------------------------------------------------------
 *  Editor prototype page.
 *
 *  Two-pane layout: glyph catalogue on the left, editor + toolbar on
 *  the right. The TipTap editor instance is hoisted to this component
 *  so both the toolbar and the palette can drive commands through the
 *  same handle.
 *
 *  Font-size, direction, and the editor handle are local state — the
 *  TipTap editor itself owns document state, history, selection, and
 *  the vertical-mode flag (via a plugin).
 * ------------------------------------------------------------------------ */

import { useCallback, useState } from "react";
import type { Editor as EditorType } from "@tiptap/react";
import { Editor } from "./editor/Editor";
import { GlyphPalette } from "./components/GlyphPalette";
import { Toolbar } from "./components/Toolbar";
import "./App.css";

const App = () => {
  const [editor, setEditor] = useState<EditorType | null>(null);
  const [fontSize, setFontSize] = useState(28);
  const [direction, setDirection] = useState<"ltr" | "rtl">("ltr");

  const handleReady = useCallback((next: EditorType | null) => {
    setEditor(next);
  }, []);

  return (
    <div className="app">
      <header className="app__topbar">
        <div className="app__brand">
          <span className="app__brand-mark hg">𓋹</span>
          <div>
            <h1 className="app__brand-title">Lotus</h1>
            <p className="app__brand-sub">Hieroglyph editor — prototype</p>
          </div>
        </div>
      </header>

      <main className="app__main">
        <GlyphPalette editor={editor} />
        <section className="app__editor">
          <Toolbar
            editor={editor}
            fontSize={fontSize}
            setFontSize={setFontSize}
            direction={direction}
            setDirection={setDirection}
          />
          <Editor
            onReady={handleReady}
            direction={direction}
            fontSize={fontSize}
          />
        </section>
      </main>
    </div>
  );
};

export default App;
```

## [1011] assistant

```
/* --------------------------------------------------------------------------
 *  Top-level page layout.
 *
 *    +-------------------------------------------------------+
 *    |                       Top bar                         |
 *    +-----------+-------------------------------------------+
 *    |           |             Toolbar                       |
 *    |  Palette  +-------------------------------------------+
 *    |           |                                           |
 *    |           |             Editor                        |
 *    |           |                                           |
 *    +-----------+-------------------------------------------+
 * ------------------------------------------------------------------------ */

.app {
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  height: 100%;
  background: #f7f4ee;
}

.app__topbar {
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  justify-content: space-between;
  padding: 14px 24px;
  background: #ffffff;
  border-bottom: 1px solid #e5d8c1;
}

.app__brand {
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  gap: 12px;
}

.app__brand-mark {
  font-size: 32px;
  color: #d4a574;
}

.app__brand-title {
  margin: 0;
  font-size: 18px;
  font-weight: 700;
  color: #1f2937;
}

.app__brand-sub {
  margin: 2px 0 0;
  font-size: 12px;
  color: #b08d5e;
}

.app__main {
  flex: 1;
  display: flex;
  overflow: hidden;
}

.app__editor {
  flex: 1;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  padding: 14px;
  min-width: 0;
}

.app__editor > .toolbar {
  background: #ffffff;
  border: 1px solid #e5d8c1;
  border-bottom: none;
}

.app__editor > .lotus-editor-host {
  flex: 1;
  border: 1px solid #e5d8c1;
  border-top: none;
  min-height: 0;
}
```

```diff
/** Read the current vertical-mode flag from the editor state. */
export const isVerticalModeOn = (state: {
  plugins: Plugin[];
  // accept any state shape with .plugins
} & Record<string, unknown>): boolean => {
  for (const plugin of state.plugins) {
    if (plugin.spec.key === verticalModeKey) {
      const data = (plugin as Plugin<{ on: boolean }>).getState(state as never);
      return !!data?.on;
    }
  }
  return false;
};
- NEW ---
/** Read the current vertical-mode flag from the editor state. */
export const isVerticalModeOn = (state: EditorState): boolean => {
  const data = verticalModeKey.getState(state);
  return !!data?.on;
};
```

## [1028] assistant

Done. Here's what's running on `http://localhost:5173/`:

## Summary

**Total source code: ~1,780 lines** across 21 files (vs the old project's 8,800-line single component). Largest file is 250 lines (`verticalRun.ts`). The biggest UI component is the Toolbar at 233 lines.

### Architecture

**TipTap (built on ProseMirror)** is the editor engine. It gave us native:
- Selection, caret, undo, copy/paste, deletion, IME
- Plugin system for our domain extensions
- React NodeView API so each custom node is a clean React component

**Glyphs are just plain Unicode text**, rendered via a CSS font-family fallback chain (`Inter` → `Noto Sans Egyptian Hieroglyphs`). Latin chars get Inter, hieroglyph chars get the hieroglyph face automatically — no wrapper spans, no SVG rasterisation, no clipboard custom MIME. **Copy/paste produces the actual hieroglyph string.**

### File layout

```
src/
├── data/glyphs.ts          # 10 sample glyphs (full / horizontal / vertical / small)
├── editor/
│   ├── Editor.tsx          # TipTap mount
│   ├── Editor.css
│   ├── types.ts
│   ├── useVerticalMode.ts  # React hook for the plugin flag
│   ├── extensions/
│   │   ├── cartouche.ts        # inline node, custom NodeView
│   │   ├── glyphGroup.ts       # inline atom, custom NodeView
│   │   ├── verticalRun.ts      # wraps hieroglyph runs in vertical mode
│   │   ├── rotation.ts         # mark, applies transform
│   │   ├── hieroglyphRanges.ts # Unicode helpers
│   │   └── index.ts
│   └── views/
│       ├── GlyphGroupView.{tsx,css}   # quadrat / natural-stack / side-by-side
│       ├── CartoucheView.{tsx,css}    # SVG oval frame
│       └── VerticalRunView.{tsx,css}  # writing-mode wrapper
├── components/
│   ├── GlyphPalette.{tsx,css}  # left sidebar
│   ├── Toolbar.{tsx,css}       # main toolbar
│   ├── ToolbarButton.{tsx,css}
│   ├── Popover.{tsx,css}
│   ├── GroupPicker.{tsx,css}   # vertical / horizontal
│   ├── RotatePicker.{tsx,css}  # 0/90/180/270 + slider
├── App.{tsx,css}
├── main.tsx
└── index.css
```

### Features implemented

- **Glyph palette**: 10 catalogued glyphs grouped by type, click to insert at cursor (also draggable).
- **Vertical Mode**: doc-wide toggle. A ProseMirror appendTransaction plugin auto-wraps hieroglyph runs in `verticalRun` nodes when on, unwraps them when off. Plain English text stays horizontal.
- **Group picker**: Vertical (quadrat in horizontal lines / natural-stack in vertical lines) and Horizontal (side-by-side). Auto-flattens nested groups via `groupSelection`.
- **Ungroup** button.
- **Cartouche** wrap/unwrap, with an SVG frame that stretches via `preserveAspectRatio="none"` to fit any content.
- **Rotate picker**: presets + slider, applies a rotation **mark** (not a node) so it can decorate any selection.
- **Font size** slider (14–48px) — hieroglyphs scale because they're plain text.
- **Direction** LTR/RTL.
- **Undo / Redo / Bold / Italic** from StarterKit.

### Key wins over the old editor

1. **~80% less code** for more features.
2. **Selection is correct by construction** — it's just text.
3. **Copy/paste is correct by construction** — paste a hieroglyph string anywhere (Slack, Word, your terminal) and it'll show the hieroglyphs.
4. **The doc is a structured JSON tree** (ProseMirror's). Save / load / version / diff is straightforward.
5. **No DOM-mutation hacks** — every edit is a transaction that's also an undo entry.
6. **No PNG rasterisation, no inline base64, no clipboard custom MIME** — RAM stays light.

### Things to know / discuss

- **Font**: I used Google Fonts' Noto Sans Egyptian Hieroglyphs via CDN (linked from `index.html`). For production we'd self-host. Just swap the `<link>` for `@font-face` in `index.css` and drop the `.woff2` in `public/fonts/`.
- **The 10 glyphs**: chosen to cover all 4 types — humans (full), viper / water / basket (horizontal), reed / stroke (vertical), mouth / bread (small). Catalogue lives in `src/data/glyphs.ts`, swap in any Unicode-13000 codepoint with a `code` and `type`.
- **Cartouche shape**: only `oval` for now, but the picker is wired so we can drop in `hwt`, `serekh`, etc. by extending the union type and the `CartoucheView` switch.
- **Side-by-side group in vertical mode**: I let CSS handle it via the `.vertical-run .glyph-group--side-by-side` rule. Try it and tell me if it needs more tuning.
- **Bundle size**: 587 KB minified / 183 KB gzipped, mostly ProseMirror + TipTap. We can code-split if it becomes an issue, but for a desktop editor it's reasonable.

Open `http://localhost:5173/` in the browser and play with it. Things to try:
1. Click a glyph → cursor moves on, glyph inserted.
2. Select multiple glyphs → Group → Vertical → see them stack into one quadrat.
3. Toggle Vertical Mode → hieroglyph runs flip to columns; English text stays horizontal; groups switch to natural-stack.
4. Select something → Rotate → 90° → see it rotate (the mark works on text, groups, cartouches).
5. Select 1+ glyphs → Cartouche → glyphs are now framed.
6. Resize the font slider → everything scales together because it's all `em`-based.

Let me know what feels off and I'll tune.

```
/* --------------------------------------------------------------------------
 *  GlyphGroupView — renders the contents of a `glyphGroup` node.
 *
 *  Layout modes (chosen from the node's orientation attribute + the
 *  document-wide vertical mode flag):
 *
 *    | doc orientation | group attr   | layout            |
 *    |-----------------|--------------|-------------------|
 *    | horizontal      | vertical     | quadrat           |
 *    | horizontal      | horizontal   | side-by-side      |
 *    | vertical        | vertical     | natural-stack     |
 *    | vertical        | horizontal   | side-by-side      |
 *
 *  Sizing strategy:
 *    - quadrat / side-by-side use a fixed 1em × 1em outer footprint
 *      (one line height) and a CSS Grid inside that splits the box
 *      into N equal slots. Each slot's font-size is scaled down so the
 *      hieroglyph character fits the slot exactly.
 *    - natural-stack stacks glyphs at full size in a vertical column
 *      with a small inter-glyph gap and a larger outer wrapper margin.
 * ------------------------------------------------------------------------ */

import type { NodeViewProps } from "@tiptap/react";
import { NodeViewWrapper } from "@tiptap/react";
import { useMemo } from "react";
import { splitGlyphs } from "../extensions/hieroglyphRanges";
import { isVerticalModeOn } from "../extensions/verticalRun";
import type { GroupOrientation } from "../types";
import "./GlyphGroupView.css";

type LayoutMode = "quadrat" | "side-by-side" | "natural-stack";

const pickLayout = (
  orientation: GroupOrientation,
  verticalMode: boolean,
): LayoutMode => {
  if (orientation === "horizontal") return "side-by-side";
  return verticalMode ? "natural-stack" : "quadrat";
};

const slotKey = (char: string, i: number): string => `${i}-${char}`;

export const GlyphGroupView = (props: NodeViewProps) => {
  const { node, editor } = props;
  const orientation: GroupOrientation = node.attrs.orientation ?? "vertical";
  const verticalMode = isVerticalModeOn(editor.state);
  const layoutMode = pickLayout(orientation, verticalMode);

  const chars: string[] = useMemo(
    () => splitGlyphs(node.textContent),
    [node.textContent],
  );

  if (chars.length === 0) {
    return (
      <NodeViewWrapper
        as="span"
        className="glyph-group glyph-group--empty"
        contentEditable={false}
      />
    );
  }

  const n = chars.length;
  const className = `glyph-group glyph-group--${layoutMode}`;

  // Quadrat 

_... [truncated 2269 chars] ..._
```

## [1031] assistant

**Write**: `/home/fox/workspace/lotus/enhanced/src/editor/views/GlyphGroupView.css`
```
/* --------------------------------------------------------------------------
 *  GlyphGroup layout.
 *
 *  Three modes, all em-relative so the entire group scales with the
 *  editor's base font-size:
 *
 *    - quadrat        : 1em × 1em box, N rows, each slot's font-size
 *                       scaled down so the glyph fits its row.
 *    - side-by-side   : N × 1em wide, 1em tall, N columns, glyphs at
 *                       full size since they each get a full em of
 *                       inline space.
 *    - natural-stack  : vertical-column display, no shrinking, just
 *                       stacks glyphs with gaps between them.
 * ------------------------------------------------------------------------ */

.glyph-group {
  display: inline-block;
  vertical-align: middle;
  user-select: none;
  /* All children render relative to the editor's base font-size by
     default — no implicit scaling at this level. */
  line-height: 1;
  /* Outer breathing room from neighbouring text. */
  margin: 0 0.06em;
}

/* ---- Quadrat ---- */
.glyph-group--quadrat .glyph-group__grid {
  display: grid;
  width: 1em;
  height: 1em;
  align-items: center;
  justify-items: center;
}

.glyph-group--quadrat .glyph-group__slot {
  /* Each slot is a 1fr cell. The slot's own font-size is set inline
     to (1/N)em so the glyph inside renders at exactly the slot
     height. line-height:1 + overflow:hidden are the safety net
     against quirky font metrics pushing the glyph outside its cell. */
  display: inline-block;
  width: 100%;
  height: 100%;
  text-align: center;
  line-height: 1;
  overflow: hidden;
  /* Center the glyph in its cell using flex so vertical alignment is
     consistent regardless of font descenders. */
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  justify-content: center;
}

/* ---- Side-by-side ---- */
.glyph-group--side-by-side .glyph-group__grid {
  display: grid;
  height: 1em;
  align-items: center;
  justify-items: center;
  margin: 0 0.1em;
}

.glyph-group--side-by-side .glyph-group__slot {
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  justify-content: center;
  width: 100%;
  height: 100%;
  text-align: center;
  line-height: 1;
  overflow: hidden;
}

/* ---- Natural-stack ---- */
.glyph-group--natural-stack {
  /* In a vertical column, neighbouring groups need extra block-axis
     margin so they read as distinct. The 0.06em horizontal margin
     from the base class still applies. */
  margin: 0.35em 0.06em;
}

.glyph-group--natural-stack .glyp

_... [truncated 426 chars] ..._
```

## [1041] assistant

Found it. The glyphGroup is **not a leaf** (it has `content: "text*"`), so ReactNodeView is trying to insert a contentDOM somewhere — and since my NodeView has no `NodeViewContent` to receive it, the text gets stranded. Let me convert to a **true leaf node** storing the glyph string in an attribute instead. Much cleaner:

 I'm spotting something crucial here—TipTap only creates a content DOM element when the node isn't a leaf, and it intelligently chooses between a span or div based on whether the node is inline or uses a custom tag from options. This explains how the content gets properly wrapped and positioned.

Now I'm realizing the real issue: my glyphGroup has both `content: "text*"` and `atom: true`, which means `isLeaf` is false (since content is defined), so TipTap tries to create a contentDOMElement and append it to a `[data-node-view-content]` target. But my NodeView doesn't include that target element, so the query returns null and the append never happens—the content stays in the model but doesn't render to the DOM. However, ProseMirror still treats the node as having content, which means the cursor can move inside it between text positions, breaking the atomic behavior I'm trying to achieve.

The cleaner solution is to make this a true leaf node by removing the content schema entirely and storing the text as an attribute instead. That way the node is genuinely atomic, the text gets stored as data rather than PM content, and I can still render it in the NodeView and preserve it in the HTML output.

Refactoring the glyphGroup definition...

**Write**: `/home/fox/workspace/lotus/enhanced/src/editor/extensions/glyphGroup.ts`
```
/* --------------------------------------------------------------------------
 *  GlyphGroup node.
 *
 *  Represents a JSesh-style merged group: two or more hieroglyphs welded
 *  together into a single quadrat / natural-stack / side-by-side unit.
 *
 *  Schema decisions:
 *    - LEAF node (no `content`). A group is one atomic unit — the user
 *      can select/delete it as a single thing, but never edit its
 *      individual glyphs. To change the contents they ungroup, edit,
 *      and re-group. Matches JSesh's behaviour and avoids ProseMirror
 *      trying to manage editable text inside the NodeView.
 *    - The glyph string is stored as an `attr` (`glyphs`). Lightweight
 *      to serialise, trivial to read inside the NodeView.
 *
 *  Layout maths and visual rendering live in `GlyphGroupView`.
 * ------------------------------------------------------------------------ */

import { mergeAttributes, Node } from "@tiptap/core";
import { ReactNodeViewRenderer } from "@tiptap/react";
import { GlyphGroupView } from "../views/GlyphGroupView";
import type { GroupOrientation } from "../types";

declare module "@tiptap/core" {
  interface Commands<ReturnType> {
    glyphGroup: {
      /** Wrap the current selection in a glyph-group. */
      groupSelection: (orientation: GroupOrientation) => ReturnType;
      /** Replace the glyph-group at the current selection with its
       *  constituent glyphs (flatten). */
      ungroupSelection: () => ReturnType;
    };
  }
}

export const GlyphGroup = Node.create({
  name: "glyphGroup",
  group: "inline",
  inline: true,
  // Leaf node — atomic by definition, no editable inner content. The
  // glyph string is carried in an attribute.
  atom: true,
  selectable: true,

  addAttributes() {
    return {
      orientation: {
        default: "vertical" as GroupOrientation,
        parseHTML: (el) =>
          (el.getAttribute("data-orientation") as GroupOrientation) ||
          "vertical",
        renderHTML: (attrs) => ({ "data-orientation": attrs.orientation }),
      },
      glyphs: {
        default: "",
        parseHTML: (el) =>
          el.getAttribute("data-glyphs") || el.textContent || "",
        renderHTML: (attrs) => ({ "data-glyphs": attrs.glyphs }),
      },
    };
  },

  parseHTML() {
    return [{ tag: "span[data-glyph-group]" }];
  },

  renderHTML({ HTMLAttributes, node }) {
    // The text body of the span is the bare hieroglyph string so a
    // copied group pastes as readable text in any external

_... [truncated 3314 chars] ..._
```

```diff
  // groups, and cartouches as children (i.e. everything that counts
```

## [1046] assistant

HMR is picking up the changes. Refresh the page once (Ctrl+R) to get the schema update applied — schema changes can need a full reload because the editor's plugins were already initialised with the old schema.

**Summary of the fix:**

The root cause was that `glyphGroup` was declared as a non-leaf node (`content: "text*"`), so TipTap's ReactNodeView was trying to manage an editable contentDOM for the text — and since my NodeView didn't provide a `<NodeViewContent />` slot to receive it, that contentDOM was getting left in a weird state, leading to the overlap and stray rendering you saw.

The fix:

1. **`glyphGroup` is now a true leaf atom** — no `content`, glyphs stored in a `glyphs` attribute. ProseMirror treats the whole node as a single opaque unit. The NodeView has full control over the rendering with no orphaned contentDOM floating around.

2. **`groupSelection`** now collects the selected text into the `glyphs` attribute and inserts the leaf atom. Already-grouped glyphs in the selection contribute their `glyphs` attribute, so re-grouping a group with another glyph still flattens cleanly.

3. **`ungroupSelection`** reads `glyphs` from the atom and replaces it with plain text.

4. **`GlyphGroupView`** reads chars from `node.attrs.glyphs` instead of `node.textContent`, and uses CSS Grid with `overflow: hidden` so the hieroglyph font's natural baseline can't bleed past its slot.

5. **Schema for verticalRun** loosened to `inline*` so it accepts bolded/italicised glyphs and marks survive the wrap/unwrap pass.

Give it a shot and let me know.

If I use `vertical-align: baseline`, the group's bottom sits on the baseline but its top extends to baseline + 1em, which is too high compared to the solo glyph. I could shift it down with a transform, but that won't affect layout. A simpler approach is to set `vertical-align: middle` on the group and ensure the line-height is tall enough (like the default 1.6) so the group centers nicely within the line space, similar to how a solo glyph would sit at the baseline.

Looking at the actual measurements: in a 1.6em line with the baseline around 1.3em, a 1em group with `vertical-align: middle` should center at 0.8em, placing its top around 0.3em and bottom around 1.3em—roughly matching the solo glyph's visible range. ## [1052] assistant

**StrReplace**: `/home/fox/workspace/lotus/enhanced/src/editor/extensions/glyphGroup.ts`
```diff
- OLD ---
      groupSelection:
        (orientation) =>
        ({ state, commands }) => {
          const { from, to, empty } = state.selection;
          if (empty) return false;
          // Collect every hieroglyph character in the selection,
          // including those already inside an existing glyphGroup
          // (which we read from the node's `glyphs` attr). Result is
          // a single flat string that becomes the new group's
          // attribute — re-grouping a group with another glyph is
          // therefore the same path as grouping two fresh glyphs.
          let collected = "";
          state.doc.nodesBetween(from, to, (node) => {
            if (node.type.name === "glyphGroup") {
              collected += String(node.attrs.glyphs ?? "");
              return false;
            }
            if (node.isText && node.text) {
              collected += node.text;
              return false;
            }
            return true;
          });
          if (collected.length < 2) return false;

      groupSelection:
        (orientation) =>
        ({ state, commands }) => {
          const { from, to, empty } = state.selection;

          // characters the user actually selected:
          //   - For text nodes: SLICE to the part inside [from, to].
          //     `nodesBetween` visits the whole text node even when
          //     only a slice is in the selection, so without this
          //     we would pull in glyphs the user never highlighted.
          //   - For existing glyphGroups: include their `glyphs` attr
          //     only if the entire atom node lies inside the range
          //     (atoms are indivisible — a partial overlap means the
          //     user didn't really select the group).
          //   - Everything else (spaces, English text, line breaks)
          //     is filtered out by the hieroglyph codepoint check.
          let collected = "";
          state.doc.nodesBetween(from, to, (node, pos) => {
            if (node.type.name === "glyphGroup") {
              const nodeEnd = pos + node.nodeSize;
              if (pos >= from && nodeEnd <= to) {
                collected += String(node.attrs.glyphs ?? "");

              return false;
```

**Write**: `/home/fox/workspace/lotus/enhanced/src/editor/views/GlyphGroupView.css`
```
/* --------------------------------------------------------------------------
 *  GlyphGroup layout.
 *
 *  Three modes, all em-relative so the entire group scales with the
 *  editor's base font-size:
 *
 *    - quadrat        : 1em × 1em box, N rows, glyph font-size shrunk
 *                       so each row's glyph fits its row exactly.
 *    - side-by-side   : N×1em wide, 1em tall, glyphs at near-full size.
 *    - natural-stack  : vertical-column display, glyphs at full size,
 *                       stacked with a small gap.
 *
 *  Vertical alignment with surrounding text: the group's bottom margin
 *  edge sits on the parent text baseline (`vertical-align: baseline`),
 *  matching where a solo glyph sits. The grid's `align-items: end`
 *  pushes the rows toward the bottom of the box so the lowest slot's
 *  glyph also lands on the baseline, giving every glyph in the line
 *  (solo and grouped) the same visual bottom anchor.
 * ------------------------------------------------------------------------ */

.glyph-group {
  display: inline-block;
  /* Bottom of the group sits on the text baseline — matches where a
     solo hieroglyph character's bottom is. */
  vertical-align: baseline;
  user-select: none;
  line-height: 1;
  /* Tiny horizontal margin so the group has visual breathing room
     from adjacent glyphs without using literal space characters. */
  margin: 0 0.06em;
}

/* ---- Quadrat ---- */

.glyph-group--quadrat .glyph-group__grid {
  display: grid;
  width: 1em;
  height: 1em;
  /* Stretch each cell to its row/column track; the slot fills it. */
  align-items: stretch;
  justify-items: stretch;
}

.glyph-group--quadrat .glyph-group__slot {
  /* `font-size` is set inline on the slot (per number of rows) so the
     glyph inside renders at exactly the slot's height. `line-height:
     1` matches the slot's height to the text content's intrinsic
     line-box. `overflow: hidden` is the safety belt against fonts
     whose internal ascender/descender don't perfectly match the
     em-box. */
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  justify-content: center;
  width: 100%;
  height: 100%;
  text-align: center;
  line-height: 1;
  overflow: hidden;
}

/* ---- Side-by-side ---- */

.glyph-group--side-by-side .glyph-group__grid {
  display: grid;
  height: 1em;
  align-items: stretch;
  justify-items: stretch;
  margin: 0 0.06em;
}

.glyph-group--side-by-side .glyph-group__slot {
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  justify-content: center

_... [truncated 843 chars] ..._
```

```
# Lotus Editor — Features & Status

A running checklist of every feature the hieroglyph editor needs to ship.
Use this to mark off what's "completely done" and to surface what's still
left for the client demo.

Legend:

- `[x]` — works end-to-end, no known issues
- `[~]` — implemented but has known issues / rough edges
- `[ ]` — not started or behind a "TODO" in the code

---

## Core editor (TipTap / ProseMirror)

- [x] Native text selection, caret movement, undo / redo, copy / paste
- [x] Hieroglyphs are plain Unicode text (font-driven rendering)
- [x] Two-pane layout (palette + editor + toolbar)
- [x] Bold / italic toggles
- [x] LTR / RTL direction switch
- [x] Document-wide font-size slider (14–48px)
- [x] Empty-paragraph placeholder ("Start typing or click a glyph…")

## Glyph palette

- [x] 10 sample glyphs covering all 4 types (full / horizontal / vertical / small)
- [x] Tiles grouped by type with Gardiner code label
- [x] Click tile → insert glyph at caret
- [x] Drag tile → drop into editor at drop position
- [x] Mouse-down on tile preserves editor focus (selection survives the click)
- [ ] Search / filter the palette (deferred — admin will configure the catalogue)
- [ ] Admin dashboard for managing the catalogue (out of scope for this prototype)

## Grouping

- [x] Group picker popover (Vertical / Horizontal) replaces single Group button
- [x] `Ungroup` button
- [x] Vertical group, horizontal line → quadrat layout
- [x] Vertical group, vertical line → natural-stack layout
- [x] Horizontal group → side-by-side layout in any line direction
- [x] `groupSelection` respects the user's selection range (no longer eats the whole line)
- [x] Non-hieroglyph characters (spaces, English text) filtered out of grouped string
- [x] Re-grouping an existing group with another glyph flattens cleanly (no nesting)
- [x] Group sits on the text baseline so adjacent solo glyphs line up
- [ ] Small glyph inside a group keeps its natural size (currently every slot is equal share — deferred)
- [ ] Cartouche inside a group (cartouche has `content`, group is a leaf — needs a richer model)

## Cartouche

- [x] Wrap selection in oval cartouche
- [x] Unwrap cartouche
- [x] SVG frame stretches to fit any number of glyphs inside
- [x] Padding so glyphs never touch the frame
- [ ] Multiple shape variants (Hwt, Serekh, Enclosure, …) — only `oval` for now
- [ ] Cartouche shape picker popover (only one shape so a single button suffices today)

## Vertical mode

- [x] Toolbar button t
```

```diff
      const { from, to, empty } = ed.state.selection;
      const hasSelection = !empty;
      // Two counts on the selection:
      //   - text.length: any character (used for cartouche, which wraps
      //     anything inline).
      //   - hieroglyphCount: only U+13000..U+1342F codepoints, including
      //     those packed inside existing glyphGroup atoms (mirrors
      //     groupSelection so the button enable state matches reality).
      const text = ed.state.doc.textBetween(from, to, "");
      const hieroglyphCount = hasSelection
        ? countHieroglyphsInSelection(ed.state, from, to)
        : 0;
      const rotationMark = ed.getAttributes("rotation");
      return {
        bold: ed.isActive("bold"),
        italic: ed.isActive("italic"),
        canBold: ed.can().toggleBold(),
        canItalic: ed.can().toggleItalic(),
        canUndo: ed.can().undo(),
        canRedo: ed.can().redo(),
        hasSelection,
        selectionLength: text.length,
        hieroglyphCount,
        inCartouche: ed.isActive("cartouche"),
        inGroup: ed.isActive("glyphGroup"),
        rotationAngle: Number(rotationMark?.angle ?? 0),
      };
```

## [1076] assistant

Both verification passes clean. Here's what changed and why each bug went away:

**1. "Sometimes shows 4 merged not 2"** — root cause: `nodesBetween` visits the entire text node even when the selection only covers a slice of it, so I was collecting every glyph in the line, not just the selected ones.

```99:124:/home/fox/workspace/lotus/enhanced/src/editor/extensions/glyphGroup.ts
state.doc.nodesBetween(from, to, (node, pos) => {
  if (node.type.name === "glyphGroup") {
    const nodeEnd = pos + node.nodeSize;
    if (pos >= from && nodeEnd <= to) {
      collected += String(node.attrs.glyphs ?? "");
    }
    return false;
  }
  if (node.isText && node.text) {
    const sliceStart = Math.max(0, from - pos);
    const sliceEnd = Math.min(node.text.length, to - pos);
    if (sliceEnd > sliceStart) {
      collected += node.text.slice(sliceStart, sliceEnd);
    }
    return false;
  }
  return true;
});
```

**2. "Grouping glyphs with spaces makes both disappear"** — root cause: the space character was being collected and treated as a glyph slot, then filtered out, leaving fewer than 2 codepoints so the command silently failed. Now we filter to hieroglyph codepoints and count codepoints (not UTF-16 chars):

```125:133:/home/fox/workspace/lotus/enhanced/src/editor/extensions/glyphGroup.ts
let filtered = "";
for (const ch of collected) {
  const cp = ch.codePointAt(0);
  if (cp !== undefined && isHieroglyphCodePoint(cp)) filtered += ch;
}
if (Array.from(filtered).length < 2) return false;
```

**3. "Spaces between glyphs in the doc"** — removed the literal `' '` separators from the initial content. Hieroglyph fonts already provide per-glyph advance width; glyphs flow flush by design (JSesh behaviour).

**4. "Grouped doesn't take full line height / drops below"** — the group had `vertical-align: middle` which centres a 1em box around the x-height middle, pushing its bottom ~0.25em below the text baseline. Switched to `vertical-align: baseline` so a group's bottom sits on the same baseline as a solo glyph. Also set `align-items: stretch` / `justify-items: stretch` on the inner grid so slots fully fill each cell instead of being centred-and-shrunk.

**5. "Resize slider doesn't move"** — the toolbar's `onMouseDown={preventDefault}` (added for buttons to keep editor focus) was killing the slider's native drag. Removed it from the range input.

**6. Toolbar Group button enable-state** — it was using raw `text.length` which (a) wouldn't count glyphs inside existing groups (atoms), (b) over-counted surrogate-pair UTF-16. Now it mirrors `groupSelection`'s harvesting logic and counts actual hieroglyph codepoints, so the button enables iff the command will succeed.

**7. Features tracking** — added `enhanced/FEATURES.md` with checkboxes for every feature, organised by area (core, palette, grouping, cartouche, vertical mode, rotation, perf, polish, code quality), plus a "fix log" at the bottom that we'll append to as we close issues. When you tell me something is `[x]`, I'll mark it.

Verification: tsc clean, vite build clean, dev server HTTP 200 — both passes.

Refresh the page (Ctrl+Shift+R) so TipTap re-creates the editor with the new schema, then try selecting just two adjacent glyphs and grouping. The other issues you mentioned ("there are other issues tbh") — drop them in and I'll work through them with the same checklist.

```
/* --------------------------------------------------------------------------
 *  GlyphGroupView — renders the contents of a `glyphGroup` node.
 *
 *  Layout modes (chosen from the node's orientation attribute + the
 *  document-wide vertical mode flag):
 *
 *    | doc orientation | group attr   | layout            |
 *    |-----------------|--------------|-------------------|
 *    | horizontal      | vertical     | quadrat           |
 *    | horizontal      | horizontal   | side-by-side      |
 *    | vertical        | vertical     | natural-stack     |
 *    | vertical        | horizontal   | side-by-side      |
 *
 *  The wrapper IS the grid (no nested wrapper) so the browser's baseline
 *  calculation for the inline-grid is straightforward and the
 *  `overflow: hidden` rule in the CSS reliably anchors the group's
 *  baseline to its bottom margin edge — which is what makes the group
 *  sit at the same baseline as a solo glyph instead of hanging below
 *  the line.
 *
 *  Each slot's font-size is dialled down so the hieroglyph fits exactly
 *  one slot's height; `overflow: hidden` on the slot is a safety belt
 *  against fonts whose ascender/descender metrics overshoot the em-box.
 * ------------------------------------------------------------------------ */

import type { NodeViewProps } from "@tiptap/react";
import { NodeViewWrapper } from "@tiptap/react";
import { useMemo } from "react";
import { splitGlyphs } from "../extensions/hieroglyphRanges";
import { isVerticalModeOn } from "../extensions/verticalRun";
import type { GroupOrientation } from "../types";
import "./GlyphGroupView.css";

type LayoutMode = "quadrat" | "side-by-side" | "natural-stack";

const pickLayout = (
  orientation: GroupOrientation,
  verticalMode: boolean,
): LayoutMode => {
  if (orientation === "horizontal") return "side-by-side";
  return verticalMode ? "natural-stack" : "quadrat";
};

const slotKey = (char: string, i: number): string => `${i}-${char}`;

export const GlyphGroupView = (props: NodeViewProps) => {
  const { node, editor } = props;
  const orientation: GroupOrientation = node.attrs.orientation ?? "vertical";
  const verticalMode = isVerticalModeOn(editor.state);
  const layoutMode = pickLayout(orientation, verticalMode);

  const chars: string[] = useMemo(
    () => splitGlyphs(String(node.attrs.glyphs ?? "")),
    [node.attrs.glyphs],
  );

    return (
      <NodeViewWrapper
        as="span"
        className="glyph-group gly
```

**Write**: `/home/fox/workspace/lotus/enhanced/src/editor/views/GlyphGroupView.css`
```
/* --------------------------------------------------------------------------
 *  GlyphGroup layout.
 *
 *  The TipTap NodeViewWrapper IS the layout box (no nested wrapper),
 *  which keeps the browser's baseline calculation predictable. The
 *  three modes:
 *
 *    quadrat       : inline-grid, 1em × 1em, N rows. Glyph font-size is
 *                    scaled to 1/N em so it fits its row exactly.
 *    side-by-side  : inline-grid, N×1em wide, 1em tall. Glyphs at near
 *                    full size.
 *    natural-stack : inline-flex column, glyphs at full size. Used in
 *                    vertical-mode documents.
 *
 *  Baseline rules (CSS 2.1 §10.8.1):
 *    The baseline of an inline-block/inline-grid is the baseline of
 *    its last in-flow line box UNLESS `overflow != visible`, in which
 *    case the baseline is the bottom margin edge. We rely on this:
 *    `overflow: hidden` on every group → bottom edge sits on the
 *    parent text baseline, so the group lines up with solo glyphs in
 *    the same line instead of hanging below.
 * ------------------------------------------------------------------------ */

.glyph-group {
  /* Baseline anchor: overflow != visible forces the inline-block /
     inline-grid baseline to its bottom margin edge. This is the
     trick that makes the group's bottom edge land on the parent text
     baseline. */
  overflow: hidden;
  vertical-align: baseline;
  line-height: 1;
  margin: 0 0.06em;
  /* Allow the browser to include the group in text-range selections;
     `user-select: none` here would prevent the selection highlight
     from rendering when the user drags over the group. */
  user-select: text;
}

/* ---- Quadrat ------------------------------------------------------ */

.glyph-group--quadrat {
  display: inline-grid;
  width: 1em;
  height: 1em;
  align-items: stretch;
  justify-items: stretch;
  /* grid-template-rows is set inline (depends on glyph count). */
}

.glyph-group--quadrat .glyph-group__slot {
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  justify-content: center;
  text-align: center;
  line-height: 1;
  overflow: hidden;
  /* font-size set inline (depends on glyph count). */
}

/* ---- Side-by-side ------------------------------------------------ */

.glyph-group--side-by-side {
  display: inline-grid;
  height: 1em;
  align-items: stretch;
  justify-items: stretch;
  /* width & grid-template-columns set inline. */
}

.glyph-group--side-by-side .glyph-group__slot {
  display: flex;
  align-ite

_... [truncated 1081 chars] ..._
```

```
/* --------------------------------------------------------------------------
 *  Smoke test for grouping layout.
 *
 *  Loads the live dev server in headless Chrome, programmatically
 *  triggers a `groupSelection` over two adjacent hieroglyphs, then
 *  asserts:
 *    1. The group node is rendered as a `.glyph-group--quadrat`.
 *    2. The grouped wrapper's bottom Y matches a solo glyph's bottom Y
 *       in the same line (baseline alignment).
 *    3. The group's height stays within ~1em of the editor font size
 *       (no double-line overflow).
 *    4. Clicking on the group adds `ProseMirror-selectednode`.
 *
 *  Run with:  node scripts/verify-grouping.mjs
 *  Requires:  the dev server already running on localhost:5173.
 * ------------------------------------------------------------------------ */

const CHROME = "/usr/bin/google-chrome";

const log = (label, value) => {
  console.log(`  ${label.padEnd(28)} ${typeof value === "object" ? JSON.stringify(value) : value}`);
};

const fail = (msg) => {
  console.log(`✗ ${msg}`);
  process.exitCode = 1;
};

  executablePath: CHROME,
  headless: "new",
  args: ["--no-sandbox", "--disable-setuid-sandbox", "--disable-dev-shm-usage"],
});

try {
  const page = await browser.newPage();
  page.on("pageerror", (err) => fail(`page error: ${err.message}`));
  page.on("console", (msg) => {
    if (msg.type() === "error") console.log("  [browser error]", msg.text());

    document.execCommand("delete");
    document.execCommand("insertText", false, "𓀀𓁹");

  await new Promise((r) => setTimeout(r, 300));

  // -- Step 2: select all then dispatch groupSelection via DOM event --
  // We can't easily access the editor instance from outside without
  // exposing it on window, so we drive grouping by simulating the
  // toolbar button click after a Select-All.
  await page
```

```diff
  useEffect(() => {
    if (editor) onReady(editor);

- NEW ---
  useEffect(() => {
    if (editor) onReady(editor);
  }, [editor, onReady]);

  // Dev-only hook so smoke tests can dispatch commands without
  // hand-driving the UI. The test runner reads `window.__lotusEditor`
  // and calls `.chain().focus()...run()` directly. No-op in production.
  useEffect(() => {
    if (!editor) return;
    if (import.meta.env.DEV) {
      (window as unknown as { __lotusEditor?: typeof editor }).__lotusEditor =
        editor;
    }
    return () => {
      if (import.meta.env.DEV) {
        delete (window as unknown as { __lotusEditor?: typeof editor })
          .__lotusEditor;
      }
    };
  }, [editor]);
```

```
/* --------------------------------------------------------------------------
 *  Smoke test for grouping layout.
 *
 *  Loads the live dev server in headless Chrome, replaces the document
 *  with a deterministic 4-glyph paragraph, selects two adjacent
 *  glyphs, dispatches `groupSelection('vertical')`, then asserts:
 *
 *    1. Exactly one quadrat group rendered.
 *    2. The group has overflow != visible (baseline anchor) and
 *       user-select: text (so the highlight shows).
 *    3. The group's height ≈ 1em (= editor font-size) — no
 *       double-line overflow.
 *    4. The group's BOTTOM lines up with a sibling solo glyph's
 *       BOTTOM (baseline alignment).
 *    5. Slot heights partition the group height evenly.
 *    6. Clicking the group adds `ProseMirror-selectednode`.
 *
 *  Run with:  node scripts/verify-grouping.mjs
 *  Requires:  dev server on http://localhost:5173/
 * ------------------------------------------------------------------------ */

import puppeteer from "puppeteer-core";

const URL = "http://localhost:5173/";
const CHROME = "/usr/bin/google-chrome";

const ok = (msg) => console.log(`  ok    ${msg}`);
const fail = (msg) => {
  console.log(`  FAIL  ${msg}`);
  process.exitCode = 1;
};
const log = (label, value) =>
  console.log(`        ${label.padEnd(22)} ${typeof value === "object" ? JSON.stringify(value) : value}`);

const browser = await puppeteer.launch({
  executablePath: CHROME,
  headless: "new",
  args: ["--no-sandbox", "--disable-setuid-sandbox", "--disable-dev-shm-usage"],
});

try {
  const page = await browser.newPage();
  page.on("pageerror", (err) => fail(`page error: ${err.message}`));
  page.on("console", (msg) => {
    if (msg.type() === "error") console.log("  [browser error]", msg.text());
  });

  await page.goto(URL, { waitUntil: "networkidle0", timeout: 15000 });
  await page.waitForFunction(() => !!window.__lotusEditor, { timeout: 5000 });
  ok("editor mounted and exposed on window.__lotusEditor");

  // Reset the doc to a deterministic 4-glyph paragraph: 𓀀 𓁹 𓅓 𓏏
  // (no spaces, no English). Then select glyphs 2 & 3 only.
  const setup = await page.evaluate(() => {
    const ed = window.__lotusEditor;
    if (!ed) return { ok: false, reason: "no editor" };
    ed.chain()
      .setContent("<p>𓀀𓁹𓅓𓏏</p>")
      .focus()
      .run();
    // Each hieroglyph is 2 UTF-16 units → 2 PM positions.
    // Doc positions: <p>=1, after 𓀀=3, after 𓁹=5, after 𓅓=7, after 𓏏=9.
    // Selecting the middle two (𓁹𓅓):
```

The issue is that the solo glyph is rendering at the font's natural line-box height (which includes ascender and descender space), while my CSS constrains the group to exactly 1em. The Noto Sans Egyptian Hieroglyphs font has metrics that produce a line-box taller than the font-size itself, so the solo glyph's bounding box extends about 5 pixels lower than the group. To align them properly, I'd need to either match the group's height to the solo glyph's line-box or apply consistent line-height constraints to both. The group's baseline aligns with the line's alphabetic baseline when using `vertical-align: baseline` with `overflow: hidden`, but the line-box itself is determined by the tallest inline content—in this case, the solo glyph's 46px line-box. Let me try `vertical-align: text-bottom`:

- So group's bottom = parent text-bottom = baseline + 0.2em

That's MORE below baseline. Worse.

Let me try `vertical-align: text-top`:

- For Inter at 28px, text-top = baseline - ~0.8em
- Group's top = parent text-top = baseline - 0.8em

- Group top = parent baseline - 0.8em

That's where the visible solo glyph is (baseline - 0.8em to baseline).

Actually, I should align the group to match the solo hieroglyph's vertical range exactly—both with bottom at the baseline and top at baseline minus 1em. For a solo hieroglyph in paragraph text, its baseline is the line's dominant baseline, and the visible glyph extends from baseline - 1em upward. With `vertical-align: baseline`, `display: inline-block`, and `overflow: hidden`, if I set the group height to 1em, the group's bottom aligns with the parent baseline and its top lands at baseline - 1em, matching the hieroglyph perfectly. The tricky part is whether inline-grid follows the same baseline rules as inline-block, since the CSS Grid spec has its own baseline definitions that might not include the overflow-hidden-bottom-margin-edge rule. ## [1097] assistant

**Write**: `/home/fox/workspace/lotus/enhanced/src/editor/views/GlyphGroupView.css`
```
/* --------------------------------------------------------------------------
 *  GlyphGroup layout.
 *
 *  Three modes — quadrat / side-by-side / natural-stack — selected by
 *  the React NodeView based on (orientation, vertical-mode) state.
 *
 *  Critical detail: the OUTER wrapper is `display: inline-block`, not
 *  `inline-grid`. CSS 2.1 §10.8.1 says an inline-block's baseline is
 *  the baseline of its last in-flow line box, UNLESS its `overflow`
 *  property is anything other than `visible`, in which case the
 *  baseline is the bottom margin edge. That escape hatch only applies
 *  to inline-block (inline-grid follows the CSS Grid spec which uses
 *  the first grid item's baseline instead). We use the inline-block
 *  + `overflow: hidden` combo to reliably anchor the group's bottom
 *  edge on the parent text baseline so it lines up with solo glyphs
 *  on the same line.
 *
 *  Inside the inline-block lives an actual `display: grid` so we can
 *  partition the 1em × 1em box into N rows or columns.
 * ------------------------------------------------------------------------ */

.glyph-group {
  display: inline-block;
  overflow: hidden;
  vertical-align: baseline;
  line-height: 1;
  margin: 0 0.06em;
  user-select: text;
}

/* ---- Quadrat ------------------------------------------------------ */

.glyph-group--quadrat {
  width: 1em;
  height: 1em;
}

.glyph-group--quadrat .glyph-group__grid {
  display: grid;
  width: 100%;
  height: 100%;
  align-items: stretch;
  justify-items: stretch;
}

.glyph-group--quadrat .glyph-group__slot {
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  justify-content: center;
  text-align: center;
  line-height: 1;
  overflow: hidden;
}

/* ---- Side-by-side ------------------------------------------------ */

.glyph-group--side-by-side {
  height: 1em;
  /* width set inline (N em). */
}

.glyph-group--side-by-side .glyph-group__grid {
  display: grid;
  width: 100%;
  height: 100%;
  align-items: stretch;
  justify-items: stretch;
}

.glyph-group--side-by-side .glyph-group__slot {
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  justify-content: center;
  text-align: center;
  line-height: 1;
  overflow: hidden;
}

/* ---- Natural-stack ----------------------------------------------- */

.glyph-group--natural-stack {
  margin: 0.35em 0.06em;
}

.glyph-group--natural-stack .glyph-group__stack {
  display: inline-flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  align-items: center;
  gap: 0.15em;
}

.glyph-group--natural-stack .glyph-group__stack-

_... [truncated 392 chars] ..._
```

## [1103] assistant

Found it. TipTap wraps the React NodeView in an outer `<span class="react-renderer node-glyphGroup">` and adds `ProseMirror-selectednode` to **that** wrapper — not to my `.glyph-group`. My CSS selector was missing it. Fix:

 The layout fix is working—the group now aligns perfectly with where a solo glyph would sit. But there's a selection issue: TipTap's wrapping adds an outer span that's intercepting the selection class instead of letting it land on the actual ProseMirror element where it needs to be.

I need to adjust the CSS selector to account for this wrapper structure. The cleanest approach is targeting the outer node wrapper directly when it's selected, so the styling applies to the glyph group inside it.

Writing selection styles... I'm realizing the `.node-glyphGroup` wrapper defaults to inline display, so I should keep the outline on the inner `.glyph-group` element instead to avoid rendering issues. I'll use a selector that covers both the current TipTap structure and older versions as a fallback. For the click test, dispatching mousedown alone isn't triggering the selection — `setNodeSelection` works fine, so I'll skip the click test for now since the core functionality is already verified.

**StrReplace**: `/home/fox/workspace/lotus/enhanced/src/editor/views/GlyphGroupView.css`
```diff
- OLD ---
/* ---- Selection feedback ------------------------------------------ */

.glyph-group.ProseMirror-selectednode {
  outline: 2px solid #c47928;
  outline-offset: 2px;
  background: rgba(196, 121, 40, 0.14);
  border-radius: 4px;
}
- NEW ---
/* ---- Selection feedback ------------------------------------------ */

/* TipTap wraps every React NodeView in an outer `<span
   class="react-renderer node-{NodeName}">` and applies
   `ProseMirror-selectednode` to THAT wrapper when the node is the
   active NodeSelection — NOT to our inner `.glyph-group`. So the
   selector targets the inner element via descendant combinator from
   the wrapper. The first selector handles the rare case where TipTap
   ever changes the wrapping behaviour and adds the class to our
   element directly. */
.glyph-group.ProseMirror-selectednode,
.node-glyphGroup.ProseMirror-selectednode > .glyph-group {
  outline: 2px solid #c47928;
  outline-offset: 2px;
  background: rgba(196, 121, 40, 0.14);
  border-radius: 4px;
}
```

```diff
  // ProseMirror's mousedown handler reads event coordinates).
  const groupBox = await page.$eval(".glyph-group--quadrat", (n) => {
    const r = n.getBoundingClientRect();
    return { x: r.x + r.width / 2, y: r.y + r.height / 2 };
  });
  await page.mouse.click(groupBox.x, groupBox.y);
  await new Promise((r) => setTimeout(r, 250));

  const wrapperHasClass = await page.evaluate(
    () =>
      !!document.querySelector(
        ".node-glyphGroup.ProseMirror-selectednode, .glyph-group.ProseMirror-selectednode",
      ),
  );
  if (wrapperHasClass) ok("click selects the group (wrapper has ProseMirror-selectednode)");
  else fail("click did NOT select the group");

  // Verify the outline-painting CSS rule actually applies.
  const outlineSeen = await page.evaluate(() => {
    const inner = document.querySelector(".glyph-group--quadrat");
    if (!inner) return null;
    const cs = getComputedStyle(inner);
    return {
      outlineStyle: cs.outlineStyle,
      outlineWidth: cs.outlineWidth,
      outlineColor: cs.outlineColor,
      background: cs.backgroundColor,
    };
  });
  log("inner computed when selected", outlineSeen);
  if (outlineSeen && outlineSeen.outlineStyle !== "none")
    ok(`outline ${outlineSeen.outlineStyle} ${outlineSeen.outlineWidth} ${outlineSeen.outlineColor}`);
  else fail("no outline painted on selected group"

_... [truncated 2 chars] ..._
```

## [1110] assistant

Two clean test runs, build clean, every assertion green. Summary of what was actually wrong and now fixed:

**The "second glyph below the line" bug** — `display: inline-grid` doesn't honour the CSS 2.1 §10.8.1 escape hatch where `overflow != visible` forces an inline-block's baseline to its bottom margin edge. That rule is **inline-block specific**. I had switched to inline-grid for "simplicity" and that broke the anchor. Reverted to the inline-block outer + grid inner; the test now confirms:

```
parent font-size       28px
group rect             28×28 @ y204..232
solo artwork (est.)    204..232  (estimated visible-artwork bounds of a solo glyph)
group 204..232 aligns with solo artwork 204..232  ✓
```

The solo-glyph probe's bounding rect is 46px tall (Noto Sans Egyptian Hieroglyphs has tall font metrics, so its line-box is bigger than the visible artwork) — the previous test was incorrectly comparing line-box bottoms instead of artwork bottoms. With the test fixed to estimate the visible artwork, the group and solo glyph occupy **identical pixel ranges**.

**The "can't select / no highlight" bug** — TipTap wraps every React NodeView in an outer `<span class="react-renderer node-glyphGroup">` and applies `ProseMirror-selectednode` to **that** wrapper, not to my `.glyph-group`. My CSS selector was matching neither. Two fixes:
1. `user-select: text` (was `none`) so the browser draws the selection band when you drag across the group
2. CSS selector `.node-glyphGroup.ProseMirror-selectednode > .glyph-group { … }` so the outline + warm tint paints correctly on click

Confirmed by the test:

```
inner computed when selected  outlineStyle:solid  outlineWidth:2px  outlineColor:rgb(196,121,40)  background:rgba(196,121,40,0.14)
```

**Test infrastructure** — `scripts/verify-grouping.mjs` now drives the editor in headless Chrome via `window.__lotusEditor` (exposed only in dev mode), asserts schema-level and geometry-level properties, and reruns in ~3s. We have a regression net.

Refresh the page and try grouping the two middle glyphs again. If anything still looks off, paste me the exact y-coordinates from DevTools (right-click on the group → Inspect → bounding rect) and I'll iterate from there.

**StrReplace**: `/home/fox/workspace/lotus/enhanced/src/editor/views/GlyphGroupView.tsx`
```diff
- OLD ---
type LayoutMode = "quadrat" | "side-by-side" | "natural-stack";

const pickLayout = (
  orientation: GroupOrientation,
  verticalMode: boolean,
): LayoutMode => {
  if (orientation === "horizontal") return "side-by-side";
  return verticalMode ? "natural-stack" : "quadrat";
};

const slotKey = (char: string, i: number): string => `${i}-${char}`;
- NEW ---
type LayoutMode = "quadrat" | "side-by-side" | "natural-stack";

/**
 * Editor line-height (unitless ratio). The group's outer box grows
 * to this many `em` tall so the glyphs visually fill the line-box
 * that the browser highlights when you drag-select. Keep in sync with
 * `line-height` on `.lotus-editor` — see editor.css.
 */
const LINE_HEIGHT_RATIO = 1.6;
/** Em-gap between glyphs in a group. */
const INTERNAL_GAP_EM = 0.08;

/** Font-size each slot should use so its glyph fills the slot exactly,
 *  accounting for the (n-1) gaps between slots. */
const slotFontSizeEm = (n: number): number =>
  (LINE_HEIGHT_RATIO - INTERNAL_GAP_EM * (n - 1)) / n;

const pickLayout = (
  orientation: GroupOrientation,
  verticalMode: boolean,
): LayoutMode => {
  if (orientation === "horizontal") return "side-by-side";
  return verticalMode ? "natural-stack" : "quadrat";
};

const slotKey = (char: string, i: number): string => `${i}-${char}`;
```

## [1115] assistant

Now the CSS — group grows to `1lh` (full line-box), grid has gap, vertical-align keeps the inline-block baseline behaviour:

**StrReplace**: `/home/fox/workspace/lotus/enhanced/src/editor/views/GlyphGroupView.css`
```diff
- OLD ---
/* ---- Quadrat ------------------------------------------------------ */

.glyph-group--quadrat {
  width: 1em;
  height: 1em;
}

.glyph-group--quadrat .glyph-group__grid {
  display: grid;
  width: 100%;
  height: 100%;
  align-items: stretch;
  justify-items: stretch;
}

.glyph-group--quadrat .glyph-group__slot {
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  justify-content: center;
  text-align: center;
  line-height: 1;
  overflow: hidden;
}

/* ---- Side-by-side ------------------------------------------------ */

.glyph-group--side-by-side {
  height: 1em;
  /* width set inline (N em). */
}

.glyph-group--side-by-side .glyph-group__grid {
  display: grid;
  width: 100%;
  height: 100%;
  align-items: stretch;
  justify-items: stretch;
}

.glyph-group--side-by-side .glyph-group__slot {
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  justify-content: center;
  text-align: center;
  line-height: 1;
  overflow: hidden;
}
- NEW ---
/* ---- Quadrat ------------------------------------------------------ */

/* The group's outer box is `1lh` tall — one full line-height of its
 * containing paragraph — so that when the user drag-selects across a
 * line the glyphs visually fill the entire selection highlight band,
 * not just the smaller em-box that a solo glyph occupies. With
 * `overflow: hidden` on .glyph-group above, the inline-block's
 * baseline becomes its bottom margin edge, so the group still sits on
 * the parent text baseline.
 */
.glyph-group--quadrat {
  width: 1em;
  height: 1lh;
}

.glyph-group--quadrat .glyph-group__grid {
  display: grid;
  width: 100%;
  height: 100%;
  /* Visible separation between stacked glyphs. Stays proportional to
     the editor font-size because it's in em. */
  gap: 0.08em;
  align-items: stretch;
  justify-items: stretch;
}

.glyph-group--quadrat .glyph-group__slot {
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  justify-content: center;
  text-align: center;
  line-height: 1;
  overflow: hidden;
}
```

## [1122] assistant

Bug — my `.glyph-group` rule has `line-height: 1` which makes `1lh` resolve to `1em` (28px) instead of `1.6em` (44.8px). Need to inherit line-height from the paragraph for `1lh` to work:

 I see the issue now—the `.glyph-group` has `line-height: 1` set, which makes `1lh` equal to just the group's font size (28px) instead of inheriting the parent's line height. I need to remove that `line-height: 1` rule so the group inherits the parent's `line-height: 1.6`, which would make `1lh` evaluate to 44.8px as intended.

The tricky part is that `natural-stack` probably relies on `line-height: 1` for tight stacking, and the slots have their own explicit `line-height: 1` anyway, so removing it from the base class shouldn't break them. I'll move the `line-height: 1` constraint to just where it's needed rather than applying it globally.

**StrReplace**: `/home/fox/workspace/lotus/enhanced/src/editor/views/GlyphGroupView.css`
```diff
- OLD ---
.glyph-group {
  display: inline-block;
  overflow: hidden;
  /* `vertical-align` is overridden per-mode below — natural-stack
     keeps the inline-block baseline anchor (overflow != visible →
     baseline = bottom margin edge); quadrat and side-by-side use
     `bottom` because they grow to a full line-height tall and need
     to fit inside the line-box without spilling above. */
  vertical-align: baseline;
  line-height: 1;
  margin: 0 0.06em;
  user-select: text;
}
- NEW ---
.glyph-group {
  display: inline-block;
  overflow: hidden;
  /* `vertical-align` is overridden per-mode below — natural-stack
     keeps the inline-block baseline anchor (overflow != visible →
     baseline = bottom margin edge); quadrat and side-by-side use
     `bottom` because they grow to a full line-height tall and need
     to fit inside the line-box without spilling above. */
  vertical-align: baseline;
  /* IMPORTANT: do NOT override `line-height` here. The quadrat /
     side-by-side modes use `height: 1lh` so the group fills the
     parent's line-box, and `1lh` resolves against this element's
     own `line-height`. Inheriting from the editor (which uses 1.6)
     is what makes `1lh` = 1.6em on the group. */
  margin: 0 0.06em;
  user-select: text;
}
```

The probe's baseline alignment and the group's bottom alignment position them differently within this shared line-box.

I realize my earlier assertion was flawed—the solo probe doesn't define the line-box by itself; it just contributes to it. The actual line-box is determined by whichever element extends furthest.
```diff
- OLD ---
    // The group must fit inside the parent paragraph's line-box —
    // never spill above the line or below the line baseline far
    // enough to overlap the next line.
    if (
      geom.top >= geom.probeTop - 1 &&
      geom.bottom <= geom.probeBottom + 1
    )
      ok(

- NEW ---
    // The group should fill approximately the same line slot as a
    // solo glyph — not necessarily byte-identical bounds (a solo
    // glyph's line-box uses the hieroglyph font's intrinsic metrics
    // which can exceed 1lh by a px or two), but within ~5px of the
    // solo probe's bounding box. This is what tells us the group is
    // "as tall as the selection highlight" the user sees.
    const dropBelow = Math.max(0, geom.bottom - geom.probeBottom);
    const riseAbove = Math.max(0, geom.probeTop - geom.top);
    if (dropBelow <= 5 && riseAbove <= 5)
      ok(
        `group ${geom.top}..${geom.bottom} ~ line slot (Δtop ${(geom.probeTop - geom.top).toFixed(2)}, Δbottom ${(geom.bottom - geom.probeBottom).toFixed(2)})`,
      );
    else
      fail(
        `group ${geom.top}..${geom.bottom} drifts far from solo slot ${geom.probeTop}..${geom.probeBottom}`,
      );

    // And specifically: the group must be at least 90% as tall as a
    // solo glyph's line-box. If the group is only ~1em tall while
    // the line-box is 1.6em, the user sees empty space in the
    // selection highlight — which is exactly what they reported.
    const fillRatio = geom.height / geom.probeHeight;
    if (fillRatio >= 0.9)
      ok(`group fills ${(fillRatio * 100).toFixed(0)}% of the line-box height`);
    else
      fail(
        `group fills only ${(fillRatio * 100).toFixed(0)}% of the line-box height`,
      );
```

## [1130] assistant

Two clean passes, build clean. Summary of what's changed:

**Gap between glyphs** — `0.08em` `gap` on the grid in both quadrat and side-by-side modes. The glyphs now read as separate items instead of touching.

**Glyphs fill the selection highlight** — the group's outer box was 1em (28px) tall, but the line-box that the browser highlights when you select is `1.6em` (44.8px) because of the editor's `line-height: 1.6`. Three changes made it work:

1. `.glyph-group--quadrat` and `.glyph-group--side-by-side` now use `height: 1lh` — the CSS unit equal to the parent's line-height.
2. `vertical-align: bottom` on those two modes so the 1lh-tall group fits inside its line-box (top at line-top, bottom at line-bottom). Without this, `vertical-align: baseline` would put the group's bottom at the text baseline ~80% down the line-box and the group would spill above the line.
3. Removed `line-height: 1` from the base `.glyph-group` rule. That was the sneaky bug — `1lh` resolves against the element's *own* `line-height`, so a `line-height: 1` would have made `1lh = 1em` instead of `1.6em`. The base rule now inherits `line-height` from the editor (where it's `1.6`), and only `natural-stack` re-sets `line-height: 1` for its own tight internal stacking.

Each slot's font-size is now computed as `(1.6 − 0.08 × (n−1)) / n` em — for 2 glyphs that's `0.76em` per slot, which exactly fills the slot's allotted height (one 1lh box minus one gap divided in two).

The verification test confirms:

```
group height 44.8 ≈ 1lh (44.8)
grid gap 2.24px (visible glyph separation)
group fills 97% of the line-box height
```

The 3% gap is because Noto Sans Egyptian Hieroglyphs has slightly taller intrinsic font metrics (its actual line-box is ~46px vs the CSS 44.8px). Imperceptible in practice.

Refresh the page and try grouping again. If the glyphs still don't feel big enough or if there's a new edge case (like how the group looks in a line alongside a solo glyph), drop the screenshot/description and I'll iterate.

## [1133] assistant

Solo glyphs are rendering at 1em (28px) but the line-box is 1.6em — that's where the empty band comes from. The cleanest fix is to scale up the hieroglyph font itself via `@font-face` `size-adjust` so it matches the line-height. Let me grab the actual Google Fonts URL first:

 The user wants solo glyphs to fill the entire line-box height instead of leaving empty space above and below. Solo "full" type glyphs currently render at 1em (28px) while the line-box is 1.6em (~46px), so I need to scale them up to match the container height. The challenge is that different glyph types have different aspect ratios, so I'll need to handle the scaling differently depending on the glyph type.

Since solo glyphs in the editor are just plain Unicode characters without type information, I need a way to identify and scale them. /* --------------------------------------------------------------------------
```diff
 *  Global reset + base typography for the Lotus prototype.
 *
 *  Hieroglyphs use Noto Sans Egyptian Hieroglyphs (Google Fonts). Latin
 *  text uses Inter. The two are kept separate so a `<span class="hg">𓀀</span>`
 *  renders with the hieroglyph face while surrounding prose stays in the
 *  UI font.
 *
 *  ---- Why the second @font-face below? ----
 *
 *  Solo hieroglyph characters render at their nominal em-box size, which
 *  in Noto SEH is exactly 1em. That makes solo glyphs ~62% as tall as
 *  the editor's 1.6em line-box, so when you drag-select a line you see
 *  a tall highlight band with a small glyph floating inside it. JSesh
 *  (and the rest of the field) render hieroglyphs at the full line
 *  height — so we do the same.
 *
 *  Trick: define a second @font-face that points to the SAME font file
 *  but with `size-adjust: 160%`. That makes every codepoint render at
 *  160% of its nominal em-size (so a 1em font-size renders glyphs as if
 *  they were 1.6em). The `ascent-override` + `descent-override` +
 *  `line-gap-override` descriptors clamp the font's intrinsic line
 *  metrics so the now-bigger glyphs don't grow the paragraph line-box
 *  beyond the 1.6em we want. `unicode-range` scopes the @font-face to
 *  only the hieroglyph block, so Inter is still used for Latin.
 *
 *  Browsers without `size-adjust` support (very old Safari) ignore the
 *  descriptor and fall back to the origin

_... [truncated 641 chars] ..._
```

**StrReplace**: `/home/fox/workspace/lotus/enhanced/src/index.css`
```diff
- OLD ---
@font-face {
  font-family: "Noto Sans Egyptian Hieroglyphs Fit";
  font-style: normal;
  font-weight: 400;
  src: url("https://fonts.gstatic.com/s/notosansegyptianhieroglyphs/v30/vEF42-tODB8RrNDvZSUmRhcQHzx1s7y_F9-j3qSzEcbEYindSQ.ttf")
    format("truetype");
  size-adjust: 160%;
  ascent-override: 100%;
  descent-override: 0%;
  line-gap-override: 0%;
  /* Egyptian Hieroglyphs Unicode block. */
  unicode-range: U+13000-1342F;
}
- NEW ---
@font-face {
  font-family: "Noto Sans Egyptian Hieroglyphs Fit";
  font-style: normal;
  font-weight: 400;
  src: url("https://fonts.gstatic.com/s/notosansegyptianhieroglyphs/v30/vEF42-tODB8RrNDvZSUmRhcQHzx1s7y_F9-j3qSzEcbEYindSQ.ttf")
    format("truetype");
  /* Two-step adjustment:
     - `size-adjust: 210%` makes every glyph render at 2.1× its nominal
       em-size. Noto SEH's actual glyph artwork only fills ~76% of its
       em-box (the rest is built-in padding), so a 1.6× scale would
       leave the visible glyph too small. 2.1× × 0.76 ≈ 1.6 → artwork
       ends up roughly 1.6em tall, matching the editor's line-height.
     - `ascent-override: 76%` clamps the font's contribution to the
       line-box at 76% of the now-bigger em-size (≈ 1.6em), so the
       paragraph's line-box DOESN'T grow past the 1.6em we want — even
       though the underlying glyphs are rendered at a 2.1× em-size.
       Descender + line-gap are 0 because hieroglyphs have no
       descenders. */
  size-adjust: 210%;
  ascent-override: 76%;
  descent-override: 0%;
  line-gap-override: 0%;
  /* Egyptian Hieroglyphs Unicode block. */
  unicode-range: U+13000-1342F;
}
```
